mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls reflect rising aggregate demand and wage-induced consumption, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS printed 131132.0 thousand persons in latest available month
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of months when PAYEMS exhibits positive momentum over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.5%, accommodative monetary policy suppresses short-term discount rates and incentivizes risk-taking, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1 percent in latest available month signals sub-0.5% threshold
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.10) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production indicate broad-based economic coordination that lifts corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 389502.0 and INDPRO at 94.0419 in latest month
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and INDPRO generates a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal underperforms the single-series RSAFS signal.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated unemployment, increases in industrial production signal productive capacity utilization without immediate wage-pressure constraints, expanding profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1 percent satisfies the elevated unemployment regime condition
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 8%, positive momentum in INDPRO fails to coincide with positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 600 thousand units signal durable construction demand that spurs upstream commodity and employment activity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 604.0 thousand units exceeds 600 threshold
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 600, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising consumer price index signals inflation pressure that reduces real corporate earnings and increases expected monetary tightening, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 226.268 in latest available month
failure_condition: Months with top-quartile month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes do not produce significantly negative 20-day forward returns (mean return not below zero, p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated unemployment and near-zero policy rates indicates a liquidity trap where monetary stimulus fails to generate productive employment, causing equity markets to discount weak final demand and producing negative 60-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1% and FEDFUNDS at 0.1% jointly satisfy trigger condition
failure_condition: The joint condition of UNRATE > 8% and FEDFUNDS < 0.5% fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a win rate below 50% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production directly correlates with near-term corporate revenue surprises, causing rapid positive repricing of the S&P 500 over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.0419 in latest available month
failure_condition: Positive changes in INDPRO fail to coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of weeks over a 2-year rolling window.
